title: Calamagrostis brachyathera
intro: Calamagrostis brachyathera, also known as feather reed grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its slender stems and feathery panicles. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calamagrostis brachyathera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Calamagrostis brachyathera is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Calamagrostis brachyathera is classified as a species within the genus Calamagrostis, which is a group of grasses in the family Poaceae. This classification is further supported by its membership in the subfamily Pooideae. The species is also categorized as a part of the tribe Poeae. Additionally, Calamagrostis brachyathera is classified as a perennial grass native to China. Its classification is aligned with the APG IV plant classification system.
